vineyards.name: Gaja
vineyards.address: Via Torino 18, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barbaresco DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOC
vineyards.owner: Gaja family (Gaia, Rossana and Giovanni Gaja)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings strictly by appointment in Barbaresco; book months ahead.
vineyards.description: The Gaja family has made wine in Barbaresco since 1859. Angelo Gaja modernised the estate from the 1960s and pushed single-vineyard Nebbiolo onto the world stage; the house now also farms in Barolo and beyond.

vineyards.name: Giacomo Conterno
vineyards.address: Localita Ornati 2, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG and Barbera d'Alba DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Traditionalist estate behind Barolo Monfortino; visits are limited and by appointment only.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1908, Giacomo Conterno is the standard-bearer of traditional Barolo, famous for the Monfortino Riserva from the Cascina Francia and Arione vineyards in Serralunga. Now led by Roberto Conterno.

vineyards.name: Vietti
vineyards.address: Piazza Vittorio Veneto 5, 12060 Castiglione Falletto CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Barbera d'Asti DOCG
vineyards.owner: Krause Holdings (Kyle Krause); historically the Vietti-Currado family
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided cellar visits, classic and experience tastings, and a vineyard trekking experience by online booking.
vineyards.description: A historic Castiglione Falletto house founded in 1919, known for single-cru Barolo (Rocche di Castiglione, Brunate, Ravera) and artist-label bottlings. Acquired by the Krause family in 2016.

vineyards.name: G.D. Vajra
vineyards.address: Via delle Viole 25, Frazione Vergne, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Winery visits and tastings on reservation, Monday to Sunday, in the high Vergne hamlet of Barolo.
vineyards.description: Established in 1972 in Vergne, the highest village in Barolo, the Vaira family farms organically and makes a fragrant, high-toned style led by Barolo Bricco delle Viole alongside a rare Langhe Riesling.

verified.address_quoted: Via delle Viole, 25 - Fraz. Vergne 12060 Barolo (CN)

vineyards.name: Pio Cesare
vineyards.address: Via Cesare Balbo 4, 12051 Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours and tastings by appointment, Monday to Saturday morning, in the historic Alba cellars inside the Roman walls.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1881 and still cellaring within Alba's ancient Roman walls, Pio Cesare is one of Piedmont's historic houses, making both classic blended Barolo and the single-vineyard Ornato.

vineyards.name: Ceretto
vineyards.address: Localita San Cassiano 34, 12051 Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings by appointment at the estate near Alba; the family also runs the Michelin-starred Piazza Duomo in Alba and the Acino glass tasting pod at Bricco Rocche.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1937 and expanded by brothers Bruno and Marcello Ceretto, the house farms organically across Barolo and Barbaresco crus and is known for the Bricco Rocche glass cube and Blange Arneis.

vineyards.name: Paolo Scavino
vineyards.address: Via Alba Barolo 157, 12060 Castiglione Falletto CN, Italy
vineyards.owner: Scavino family (Enrico Scavino with daughters Enrica and Elisa)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment at Castiglione Falletto.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1921, Paolo Scavino is a modern-classic Castiglione Falletto estate famed for the single-vineyard Bric del Fiasc and a portfolio of Barolo crus across the zone.

vineyards.name: Poderi Aldo Conterno
vineyards.address: Localita Bussia 48, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.owner: Aldo Conterno family (Franco, Stefano and Giacomo Conterno)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment in the Bussia hamlet at the heart of Monforte.
vineyards.description: Aldo Conterno split from the family firm in 1969 to found this Bussia estate, now a Monforte benchmark for the Granbussia Riserva and the Cicala, Colonnello and Romirasco single-vineyard Barolos.

vineyards.name: Massolino
vineyards.address: Piazza Cappellano 8, 12050 Serralunga d'Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in the village of Serralunga d'Alba.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1896, Massolino is a Serralunga d'Alba mainstay producing structured, classic Barolo led by the Vigna Rionda Riserva from one of the zone's greatest crus.

vineyards.name: Produttori del Barbaresco
vineyards.address: Via Torino 54, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe Nebbiolo DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Wine shop and tastings in the main square of Barbaresco, open to the public.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1958, this grower cooperative bottles classic Barbaresco from member vineyards, releasing nine single-cru Riserva bottlings in strong vintages alongside its village Barbaresco.

vineyards.name: Cavallotto Tenuta Bricco Boschis
vineyards.address: Via Alba-Monforte 104, Bricco Boschis, 12060 Castiglione Falletto CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Estate tastings by appointment on the Bricco Boschis hill above Castiglione Falletto.
vineyards.description: A family estate farming the Bricco Boschis monopole since the 1940s, Cavallotto makes traditional, long-aged Barolo with organic certification and is a benchmark for Castiglione Falletto.

verified.address_quoted: Via Alba-Monforte 104, Bricco Boschis, 12060 Castiglione Falletto CN

vineyards.name: Elvio Cogno
vineyards.address: Localita Ravera 2, 12060 Novello CN, Italy
vineyards.owner: Cogno-Fissore family (Valter Fissore and Nadia Cogno)
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment at the 18th-century farmhouse atop Bricco Ravera in Novello.
vineyards.description: Elvio Cogno left Marcarini in 1990 to found this Novello estate on the Ravera cru. Now run by son-in-law Valter Fissore, it is also a champion of the revived local white grape Nascetta.

vineyards.name: Luciano Sandrone
vineyards.address: Via Pugnane 4, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy
vineyards.owner: Sandrone family (Barbara Sandrone and Luca Sandrone)
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment between Barolo and Castiglione Falletto.
vineyards.description: Luciano Sandrone started bottling his own Barolo in 1978 and built one of the Langhe's most admired modern estates, led by the Cannubi Boschis cru, renamed Aleste from the 2013 vintage.

vineyards.name: Renato Ratti
vineyards.address: Frazione Annunziata 7, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Barbera d'Alba DOC and Langhe DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and visits by appointment beside the historic Abbey of the Annunziata in La Morra.
vineyards.description: Renato Ratti pioneered single-vineyard Barolo at Marcenasco in 1965 and mapped the zone's crus. The La Morra estate, now led by son Pietro, sits by the 15th-century Annunziata abbey.

vineyards.name: Fontanafredda
vineyards.address: Via Alba 15, 12050 Serralunga d'Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Alta Langa DOCG and Langhe DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours, tastings and dining at the Villaggio Narrante estate village, including the Michelin-starred Guido restaurant.
vineyards.description: Born in 1858 from the estate of King Vittorio Emanuele II, Fontanafredda is the largest contiguous wine estate in the Langhe, farming certified-organic vineyards in Serralunga d'Alba.

vineyards.name: Marchesi di Barolo
vineyards.address: Via Roma 1, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided tours of the historic cellars and tastings daily, by reservation, opposite the castle of Barolo.
vineyards.description: Rooted in the cellars where the modern Barolo style was shaped under the Falletti marquises, Marchesi di Barolo is the village's most historic house, now run by the Abbona family.

vineyards.name: Michele Chiarlo
vineyards.address: Strada Nizza-Canelli 14, 14042 Calamandrana AT, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Nizza DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Gavi DOCG
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings at the Calamandrana cellar and the open-air Art Park La Court; visits by reservation.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1956, Michele Chiarlo is one of Monferrato's leading houses, with holdings across Barbera d'Asti, Nizza, Gavi and Barolo and the La Court vineyard art park near Asti.

vineyards.name: Braida
vineyards.address: Via Roma 94, 14030 Rocchetta Tanaro AT, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Nizza DOCG and Monferrato DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided tastings and cellar visits at Rocchetta Tanaro, with a wine resort on the estate.
vineyards.description: The Bologna family has made Barbera at Rocchetta Tanaro since 1961. Giacomo Bologna's barrique-aged Bricco dell'Uccellone (first 1982) redefined Barbera as a serious, age-worthy red.

vineyards.name: La Spinetta
vineyards.address: Via Annunziata 17, 14054 Castagnole delle Lanze AT, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barbaresco DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Barbera d'Asti DOCG
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by reservation at Castagnole delle Lanze, usually mid-morning or afternoon.
vineyards.description: The Rivetti family began with Moscato in 1977 and moved into single-vineyard Barbaresco and Barolo, marking its top crus with the rhinoceros label. Known for a polished, modern style.

vineyards.name: Coppo
vineyards.address: Via Alba 68, 14053 Canelli AT, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barbera d'Asti DOCG, Nizza DOCG, Asti DOCG and Alta Langa DOCG
vineyards.tasting_program: Guided visits and tastings in the UNESCO-listed underground cathedral cellars beneath Canelli.
vineyards.description: Founded in Canelli in 1892, Coppo is a Monferrato house known for the barrique-aged Pomorosso Barbera and traditional-method sparkling wines, cellaring in UNESCO-listed tufa caves.

vineyards.name: Roberto Voerzio
vineyards.address: Localita Cerreto 7, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Small low-yield estate; visits strictly by appointment in La Morra.
vineyards.description: Roberto Voerzio set up on his own in 1986 and became famous for radically low yields and meticulous viticulture, producing concentrated, sought-after single-cru Barolo from La Morra.

vineyards.name: Elio Altare
vineyards.address: Frazione Annunziata 51, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment at Cascina Nuova in the Annunziata hamlet of La Morra.
vineyards.description: Elio Altare led the Barolo Boys modernist movement from the 1970s, introducing rotary fermenters and barrique ageing at Cascina Nuova. The La Morra estate is now run by daughter Silvia.

vineyards.name: Domenico Clerico
vineyards.address: Localita Manzoni 67, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Modern winery with tastings by appointment in the Manzoni hamlet of Monforte d'Alba.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1976, Domenico Clerico was a leading Barolo modernist, building a sleek Monforte cellar and crafting concentrated single-vineyard Barolo such as Ciabot Mentin and Pajana.

vineyards.name: Bartolo Mascarello
vineyards.address: Via Roma 15, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Tiny traditionalist cellar in the village of Barolo; visits by appointment only.
vineyards.description: A small, fiercely traditional Barolo house in the village itself, now led by Maria Teresa Mascarello, producing a single blended Barolo from four crus and the famous hand-drawn labels.

vineyards.name: Giuseppe Mascarello e Figlio
vineyards.address: Via Borgonuovo 108, 12060 Monchiero CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Historic cellar in Monchiero; visits and tastings by appointment.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1881, Giuseppe Mascarello e Figlio is renowned for the Monprivato cru in Castiglione Falletto, a benchmark of traditional, long-lived Barolo, cellared today at Monchiero.

vineyards.name: Cordero di Montezemolo
vineyards.address: Frazione Annunziata 67, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and direct sales at the Monfalletto estate in La Morra, beneath its landmark cedar of Lebanon.
vineyards.description: One of the oldest landowning families in the Langhe, Cordero di Montezemolo farms the Monfalletto estate in La Morra and the Villero cru, making refined, classically styled Barolo.

vineyards.name: Poderi e Cantine Oddero
vineyards.address: Borgata Tetti 28, Frazione Santa Maria, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Langhe DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by appointment in the Santa Maria hamlet of La Morra.
vineyards.description: A historic La Morra estate bottling since the 19th century, Oddero owns parcels in many of the greatest Barolo and Barbaresco crus and works to organic certification.

verified.address_quoted: Borgata Tetti, 28 - Frazione Santa Maria, La Morra

vineyards.name: Marchesi di Gresy
vineyards.address: Strada della Stazione 21, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy
vineyards.owner: Cisa Asinari dei Marchesi di Gresy family (Alberto di Gresy)
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and direct sales by appointment at the Martinenga estate in Barbaresco.
vineyards.description: The Gresy family has owned the Martinenga monopole in Barbaresco since 1797 and has bottled its own wine since 1973, making elegant, perfumed Nebbiolo from this prized amphitheatre.

vineyards.name: Albino Rocca
vineyards.address: Strada Ronchi 18, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy
vineyards.owner: Rocca family (Daniela, Monica and Paola Rocca)
vineyards.tasting_program: Family winery offering guided visits and tastings in Barbaresco.
vineyards.description: A family Barbaresco house developed under Angelo Rocca and now run by his daughters, making polished single-cru Nebbiolo from Ovello, Ronchi and other Barbaresco sites.

vineyards.name: Prunotto
vineyards.address: Corso Barolo 14, 12051 Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Barbaresco DOCG and Barbera d'Asti DOCG
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits to the Cantina di Bussia in Monforte d'Alba by reservation, Friday to Monday.
vineyards.description: A historic Alba house dating to 1904 and owned by Marchesi Antinori since the 1990s, Prunotto makes single-vineyard Barolo and Barbaresco plus benchmark Barbera d'Asti.

vineyards.name: Giacomo Borgogno e Figli
vineyards.address: Via Gioberti 1, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Tours and tastings in the historic Barolo-village cellars, with a panoramic terrace; visits via the booking platform.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1761, Borgogno is one of the oldest Barolo houses, known for traditional long ageing and a deep library of Riserva bottlings, in the heart of Barolo village.

vineyards.name: E. Pira e Figli - Chiara Boschis
vineyards.address: Via Vittorio Veneto 1, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Small organic estate in Barolo village; visits and tastings by appointment.
vineyards.description: Chiara Boschis took over the historic E. Pira cellar in 1990, becoming one of the Langhe's leading women winemakers and farming organically across Cannubi, Mosconi and other crus.

vineyards.name: La Scolca
vineyards.address: Strada per Rovereto 170/r, 15066 Gavi AL, Italy
vineyards.classification: Gavi DOCG and Alta Langa DOCG
vineyards.tasting_program: Tastings and visits by appointment at the Rovereto di Gavi estate, with Villa Scolca for events.
vineyards.description: The Soldati family pioneered premium Gavi at Rovereto, trademarking Gavi dei Gavi in 1969. La Scolca makes age-worthy 100% Cortese whites and traditional-method sparkling wines.

vineyards.name: Matteo Correggia
vineyards.address: Via Santo Stefano Roero 124, 12043 Canale CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Roero DOCG, Roero Arneis DOCG and Barbera d'Alba DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Family Roero estate at Canale; tastings and visits by appointment.
vineyards.description: A reference estate of the Roero, founded by Matteo Correggia in 1985 and continued by his family, making organically farmed Arneis whites and structured Nebbiolo-based Roero reds.

verified.address_quoted: Via Santo Stefano Roero, 124, 12043 Canale d'Alba (CN)

vineyards.name: Bruno Rocca
vineyards.address: Strada Rabaja 60, 12050 Barbaresco CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Cellar visits and tastings by reservation on the Rabaja hill in Barbaresco.
vineyards.description: Bruno Rocca began bottling in 1978 and built a leading Barbaresco estate on the Rabaja cru, making refined, structured Nebbiolo now with the next generation in the cellar.

vineyards.name: Cantine Damilano
vineyards.address: Via Roma 31, 12060 Barolo CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Wine shop and tastings daily on Via Roma in Barolo; cru-focused experiences by booking.
vineyards.description: A Barolo family house since 1890, Damilano is closely associated with the Cannubi cru and runs a busy tasting shop in the centre of Barolo village.

vineyards.name: Ettore Germano
vineyards.address: Localita Cerretta 1, 12050 Serralunga d'Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Barolo DOCG, Alta Langa DOCG and Langhe DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Visits and tastings by appointment on the Cerretta cru in Serralunga d'Alba.
vineyards.description: Five generations on the Cerretta cru in Serralunga, the Germano family makes structured Barolo plus a notable Langhe Riesling and Alta Langa sparkling from higher-elevation plots.

vineyards.name: Sottimano
vineyards.address: Loc. Cotta, 12052 Neive CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Family Barbaresco estate in the Cotta district of Neive; visits and tastings by appointment.
vineyards.description: Based in the Cotta district of Neive since the mid-1970s, Sottimano makes detailed single-cru Barbaresco from Cotta, Pajore, Curra and Fausoni in a fresh, transparent style.

vineyards.name: Trediberri
vineyards.address: Borgata Torriglione 4, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Small La Morra estate; visits on Mondays by appointment only.
vineyards.description: Founded in 2007 by the Vola family, Trediberri is a young La Morra estate that has quickly won acclaim for value-driven, fragrant Barolo from Berri and Rocche dell'Annunziata.

vineyards.name: Ca' del Baio
vineyards.address: Via Ferrere 33, 12050 Treiso CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Family estate in Treiso; cellar visits and tastings by appointment.
vineyards.description: A long-established Treiso family farm near Barbaresco, Ca' del Baio is run by the Grasso sisters and makes classic, well-priced single-cru Barbaresco from Asili, Pora and Vallegrande.

vineyards.name: Mauro Veglio
vineyards.address: Cascina Nuova 50, Frazione Annunziata, 12064 La Morra CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: La Morra estate at Cascina Nuova in Annunziata; visits and tastings by appointment.
vineyards.description: Mauro Veglio began estate-bottling in 1992 at Cascina Nuova in La Morra, making modern, polished single-vineyard Barolo from Arborina, Gattera and Rocche dell'Annunziata.

verified.address_quoted: Cascina Nuova, 50 Fraz. Annunziata, La Morra CN

vineyards.name: Conterno Fantino
vineyards.address: Via Ginestra 1, 12065 Monforte d'Alba CN, Italy
vineyards.tasting_program: Hilltop Monforte winery with a tasting room overlooking the vineyards; visits by reservation.
vineyards.description: Founded in 1982 by the Conterno and Fantino families, this Monforte estate farms organically on the Ginestra and Mosconi hills and makes modern, age-worthy single-cru Barolo.

vineyards.name: Pecchenino
vineyards.address: Borgata Valdiberti 59, 12063 Dogliani CN, Italy
vineyards.classification: Dogliani DOCG, Barolo DOCG and Langhe DOC
vineyards.tasting_program: Family estate at Dogliani; cellar visits and tastings by appointment.
vineyards.description: A Dogliani family estate that helped raise Dolcetto's profile with its San Luigi and Siri d'Jermu bottlings, and that also makes Barolo from a Monforte parcel.
